page: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64-based server instance powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, the instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA T4G GPU from the Turing family, featuring 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, though it does not include any local NVMe storage.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48,574.86 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,551.0. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 952.0 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 20.17 GB/sec. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is high at 1,892.97 GB/sec, the Passmark memory score falls into the weak category. For graphics and parallel processing, the instance achieves an average Geekbench ray tracing score of 26,341.0 and an average image processing score of 7,812.0.

The combination of dedicated ARM64 cores and an NVIDIA GPU makes this instance qualitatively cost-efficient for specialized workloads that benefit from GPU acceleration on ARM architecture, such as Android game streaming, machine learning inference, and video transcoding. In LLM inference tests, the instance achieves an average text generation speed of 15.24 tokens/sec for medium 7B models, though text generation for smaller 135M models is poor at 42.38 tokens/sec. The lack of local storage requires relying on network-attached storage, which should be factored into the overall architecture for database or high-I/O applications.
description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server instance featuring 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of system memory and a single NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps with no local storage. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and graphics performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 11,551.0 and a ray tracing score of 26,341.0, alongside weaker single-core metrics. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native GPU-accelerated workloads, such as Android game streaming, lightweight machine learning inference, and video transcoding, though high-I/O tasks will require network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU, optimized for cost-efficient graphics and machine learning inference workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5g.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4G GPU for accelerated workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based server featuring dedicated Graviton cores and NVIDIA GPU acceleration for cost-efficient graphics, streaming, and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores at 2.5 GHz
1.0 NVIDIA T4G GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
64.0 GB system memory and 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
Best suited for ARM-native graphics, video transcoding, and ML inference
